title: Twenty Gigabytes Is Not Enough (Tern Vision)
summary: A computer-vision inference service fails to start new Fargate tasks Friday morning. Existing tasks run fine. The fix is one missing field in the task definition.

glossary.Fargate ephemeral storage: Default 20 GiB per task on platform 1.4.0+; shared by image layers, container root filesystem, and /tmp; expanded via ephemeralStorage.sizeInGiB (min 21, max 200).
glossary.ephemeralStorage.sizeInGiB: Task-definition field that provisions additional ephemeral storage; omit the block to get the 20 GiB default; cannot be set to 20 (minimum configurable value is 21).
glossary.platform version 1.4.0: Fargate Linux runtime version that introduced 20 GiB ephemeral storage and containerd-based image pulling.
glossary.CannotPullContainerError: ECS task stoppedReason prefix emitted when the container agent cannot pull an image; for ephemeral-storage exhaustion on platform 1.4.0+ the message includes a containerd snapshotter path and 'no space left on device'.
glossary.layer extraction: Process where containerd decompresses each OCI image layer onto ephemeral storage before the container starts; peak disk usage is compressed-size-in-ECR plus uncompressed-size on disk.
glossary.sidecar container: A secondary container in the same task definition, typically for logging/tracing; its image layers also consume ephemeral storage.

system.what_broke: The task definition for tern-inference-task-def omits the ephemeralStorage block, so Fargate tasks get the 20 GiB default; the combined uncompressed size of tern-vision-inference:v2.3.1 (~14.2 GiB extracted) plus datadog-agent:7 (~2.2 GiB extracted) plus /tmp overhead exceeds 20 GiB, causing the image pull to fail with no-space-left errors.
system.data_flow: Inference requests arrive via API Gateway -> ECS Fargate service -> tern-inference tasks -> results returned; model weights bundled in the ECR image.

components.name: tern-inference-task-def (ECS task definition)
components.role: Defines both containers and launch config; no ephemeralStorage block.
components.failure_impact: New task launches fail at image-pull stage when total extracted layer size exceeds 20 GiB default.

components.name: tern-inference-service (ECS service)
connections: publishes RunningTaskCount and PendingTaskCount to ECS/ContainerInsights when Container Insights enabled
components.failure_impact: PendingTaskCount spikes, RunningTaskCount drops as in-flight replacements stall.

components.name: tern-vision-ecr-primary (ECR repository)
components.role: Stores tern-vision-inference:v2.3.1 (6.2 GiB compressed, ~14.2 GiB extracted).
connections: pulled by Fargate task execution role
components.failure_impact: v2.3.1 is the first image version where model weights push total extracted size past 20 GiB.

components.name: tern-datadog-sidecar-ecr (ECR repository)
components.role: Stores datadog-agent:7 (900 MB compressed, ~2.2 GiB extracted).
connections: pulled alongside primary image by same task
components.failure_impact: Even a small sidecar contributes ~2.2 GiB extracted, which tips the combined total over the 20 GiB limit after the v2.3.1 primary image upgrade.

progressive_clues: New Fargate tasks stopped immediately after a task definition update. Existing running tasks are unaffected.
The stoppedReason for every new task contains 'CannotPullContainerError' and 'no space left on device'. The ECR repository and network connectivity are both healthy.
Fargate platform 1.4.0 tasks get 20 GiB ephemeral storage by default. That space holds all image layers during pull, not just the container root filesystem.
Check the combined uncompressed size of all container images in the task definition. The ECR image manifest lists layer sizes in compressed form; the extracted size on disk is larger.
The task definition does not include an ephemeralStorage block. Adding ephemeralStorage: { sizeInGiB: 40 } to the task definition and deploying a new revision will give tasks enough headroom.

resolution.root_cause: tern-inference-task-def omits ephemeralStorage, so tasks receive the 20 GiB Fargate default; the v2.3.1 primary image and Datadog sidecar together require ~16.2 GiB extracted plus write-layer overhead, exhausting the default allocation before the sidecar finishes pulling.

fix_criteria.description: Player identifies stoppedReason contains 'no space left on device' in ecs-events.json.
Player calculates that combined extracted layer sizes (~16.2 GiB) plus overhead exceed 20 GiB default.
Player identifies task definition lacks ephemeralStorage field.
Player proposes adding ephemeralStorage: { sizeInGiB: <value >= 40> } to the task definition.

learning_objectives: Fargate platform 1.4.0 tasks get 20 GiB ephemeral storage by default; the minimum configurable increase is 21 GiB; 40 GiB is a safe working value for large-model inference images.
Ephemeral storage holds compressed and uncompressed image layers during pull, container writable layer, and /tmp; peak usage can be 2-3x the compressed ECR size.
How to size ephemeral storage: sum all container image compressed sizes, multiply by ~2.5 for extraction overhead, add /tmp reserve, then round up to the next 5 GiB.
EphemeralStorageUtilized (namespace ECS/ContainerInsights, dimension ClusterName + TaskDefinitionFamily) tracks usage; only available when Container Insights is enabled on the cluster.
sop_steps: Confirm stoppedReason via ECS DescribeTasks: filter stopped tasks launched after the task definition update; check stoppedReason for 'no space left on device'. If CannotPullContainerError with a containerd snapshotter path appears, disk exhaustion during pull is confirmed.

Calculate required storage: from the ECR image manifest, sum compressed layer sizes for every container in the task definition. Multiply by ~2.5 to estimate extracted size (compressed layers are typically 40-50% of uncompressed). Add 2 GiB for the container writable layer and /tmp. For tern-vision-inference:v2.3.1 (6.2 GiB compressed, ~14.2 GiB extracted) plus datadog-agent:7 (900 MB compressed, ~2.2 GiB extracted), total extracted ~16.2 GiB + ~2 GiB overhead = ~18.2 GiB; round up to 40 GiB for headroom.

Update the task definition: register a new revision of tern-inference-task-def with '"ephemeralStorage": { "sizeInGiB": 40 }' at the task level (not inside containerDefinitions). Verify requiresCompatibilities includes FARGATE and networkMode is awsvpc.
Deploy the new revision: update the ECS service to use the new task definition revision. Watch PendingTaskCount drop and RunningTaskCount recover to desiredCount in the ECS console or CloudWatch.

Enable Container Insights on the cluster if not already on ('aws ecs update-cluster-settings --cluster tern-vision-prod --settings name=containerInsights,value=enabled'). After tasks start, verify EphemeralStorageUtilized (namespace ECS/ContainerInsights) stays well below the new limit. Set a CloudWatch alarm at 80% of sizeInGiB.

related_failure_modes.scenario: Task OOM kill instead of pull failure.
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: OOM is an exit-code-137 event after the task starts; ephemeral exhaustion stops the task before any container process runs.
related_failure_modes.prevention: Set memory and memoryReservation on each container explicitly; do not rely solely on task-level memory.

related_failure_modes.scenario: ECR image pull throttled by authentication failure.
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: stoppedReason is a permissions or auth error, not 'no space left on device'; the ECR repository and task execution role are the investigation surface.
related_failure_modes.prevention: Ensure the task execution role has ecr:GetAuthorizationToken, ecr:BatchCheckLayerAvailability, ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer, ecr:BatchGetImage.

related_failure_modes.scenario: Ephemeral storage exhausted by application writes at runtime, not image pull.
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: Task starts successfully; it fails later with 'no space left on device' inside application code writing to /tmp or the container writable layer.
related_failure_modes.prevention: Same ephemeralStorage increase applies, but add monitoring via EphemeralStorageUtilized and consider moving large writes to an EFS volume or S3.

sop_practices: Size ephemeral storage before deploying a new image version, not after a production outage. Use 'aws ecr describe-images --query imageDetails[0].imageSizeInBytes' to get compressed size, then apply the 2.5x heuristic.

Enable Container Insights on every cluster where large images run. EphemeralStorageUtilized (namespace ECS/ContainerInsights) is the only vended CloudWatch metric for per-task disk usage; without it you learn about exhaustion only from stoppedReason strings.
Keep a buffer: set sizeInGiB to at least 1.5x your calculated peak. Model weights grow with retraining cycles; a buffer avoids repeat incidents on the next image version bump.
